factors.summary: Demand trends are decelerating due to unfavorable weather and inventory levels.

evidence.text: Wet and cold weather delayed pool construction activity in several key markets during the three months ended March 31, 2019. In addition, we have seen moderating growth in several of our end markets, primarily in our Filtration Solutions end markets in Europe.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Demand trends are decelerating due to unfavorable weather and inventory levels.

factors.summary: Order backlog is stable as inventory levels are not being reduced as anticipated.

evidence.text: As a result of slower sell-through during the first quarter of 2019, inventory levels were not reduced to the levels we originally anticipated. We expect inventory levels in the channel to come down as stronger sell-through resumes in the second and third quarters of 2019.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Order backlog is stable despite slower sell-through and inventory levels not being reduced as anticipated.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices to mitigate inflationary cost increases.

evidence.text: We strive for productivity improvements, and we implement increases in selling prices to help mitigate this inflation. We expect the current economic environment will result in continuing price volatility for many of our raw materials, and we are uncertain as to the timing and impact of these market changes.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is successfully raising prices to mitigate inflationary cost increases.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting toward lower-margin segments due to decreased sales in higher-margin businesses.

evidence.text: The 0.4 percentage point decrease in gross profit as a percentage of sales in the first quarter of 2019 from 2018 was primarily driven by: •unfavorable mix as a result of a 6.4 percent core growth decrease in the higher margin Aquatic Systems segment; and •inflationary increases related to labor costs and certain raw materials.
evidence.subsection: Gross profit

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix is shifting toward lower-margin segments due to decreased sales in higher-margin businesses.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating due to decreased sales volumes and unfavorable weather conditions.

evidence.text: The 6.0 percentage point decrease in net sales in the first quarter of 2019 from 2018 was primarily driven by: •decreased sales volume in our Aquatic Systems segment and agriculture-related business in our Flow Technologies segment due to cold and wet weather and higher than anticipated inventory levels in some of our key distribution channels; and •unfavorable foreign currency effects for the three months ended March 31, 2019.
evidence.subsection: Net sales

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is decelerating due to decreased sales volumes and unfavorable weather conditions.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are declining due to unfavorable weather and inventory levels.

evidence.text: The 8.3 percent decrease in net sales for Aquatic Systems in the first quarter of 2019 from 2018 was primarily driven by: •sales volume declines due to cold, wet weather and higher than anticipated inventory levels in some of our key distribution channels impacting our residential and commercial businesses; •the impact of divestitures; and •unfavorable foreign currency effects compared to the same period of the prior year.
evidence.subsection: Aquatic Systems

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are declining due to unfavorable weather and inventory levels.

factors.summary: Residential and commercial markets are slowing, while industrial and food/beverage markets are stable.

evidence.text: We have seen moderating growth in several of our end markets, primarily in our Filtration Solutions end markets in Europe. The inclement weather also impacted our higher margin Specialty Ag Spray business within our Flow Technologies segment.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Residential and commercial markets are slowing, while industrial and food/beverage markets are stable.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising due to inflationary increases in labor and raw materials.

evidence.text: We have experienced material and other cost inflation. We strive for productivity improvements, and we implement increases in selling prices to help mitigate this inflation. We expect the current economic environment will result in continuing price volatility for many of our raw materials, and we are uncertain as to the timing and impact of these market changes.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Input costs are rising due to inflationary increases in labor and raw materials.

factors.summary: Labor costs are rising, contributing to margin pressure.

evidence.text: The 0.7 percentage point increase in segment income for Filtration Solutions as a percentage of net sales in the first quarter of 2019 from 2018 was primarily driven by: •selective increases in selling prices to mitigate inflationary cost increases; •increased productivity and positive mix; and •the impact of the Aquion and Pelican acquisitions. The increase was partially offset by: •inflationary increases related to labor costs and certain raw materials.
evidence.subsection: Filtration Solutions

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Rising labor costs contribute to margin pressure, indicating a moderate headwind.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are compressing due to lower sales and higher costs.

evidence.text: The 0.4 percentage point decrease in gross profit as a percentage of sales in the first quarter of 2019 from 2018 was primarily driven by: •unfavorable mix as a result of a 6.4 percent core growth decrease in the higher margin Aquatic Systems segment; and •inflationary increases related to labor costs and certain raw materials.
evidence.subsection: Gross profit

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Gross and operating margins are compressing due to lower sales and higher costs, showing significant deterioration.

factors.summary: Cost reduction initiatives are ongoing but evidence of measurable savings is limited.

evidence.text: We are reinforcing our businesses to more effectively address these opportunities through research and development and additional sales and marketing resources. Unless we successfully penetrate these markets, our core sales growth will likely be limited or may decline.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Cost reduction initiatives are ongoing but lack measurable savings, showing mixed signals.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving slightly due to lean and supply management practices.

evidence.text: This decrease was partially offset by: •selective increases in selling prices to mitigate impacts of inflation; and •increased productivity.
evidence.subsection: Gross profit

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity is improving slightly, indicating a moderate tailwind.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are higher than anticipated, posing a risk of excess stock.

evidence.text: As a result of slower sell-through during the first quarter of 2019, inventory levels were not reduced to the levels we originally anticipated. We expect inventory levels in the channel to come down as stronger sell-through resumes in the second and third quarters of 2019.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Higher inventory levels pose a risk of excess stock, indicating a moderate headwind.

factors.summary: Potential regulatory changes could impact the effective tax rate and financial performance.

evidence.text: Proposed regulations as part of the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act, enacted in the U.S. in December 2017, may place limitations on the deductibility of certain interest expense for U.S. tax purposes. These proposed regulations could materially adversely affect our financial condition, results of operations, cash flows or our effective tax rate in future reporting periods when enacted.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Potential regulatory changes could significantly impact financial performance, indicating a major risk.

factors.summary: Forward guidance is cautious, expecting stronger sell-through in future quarters.

evidence.text: We expect inventory levels in the channel to come down as stronger sell-through resumes in the second and third quarters of 2019. Our financial results for future quarters may be negatively impacted if stronger inventory sell-through or end market growth does not resume in future quarters.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Forward guidance is cautious with expectations of stronger sell-through, showing mixed signals.

factors.summary: Management is showing cautious confidence through investments in growth areas.

evidence.text: Our operating objectives include the following: •Delivering our growth priorities through new products and global and market expansion, specifically in the areas of pool and residential and commercial water treatment especially through acquisitions and focus on China and Southeast Asia; and •Optimizing our technological capabilities to increasingly generate innovative new products and advance digital transformation.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: positive

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are increasing to support productivity and growth initiatives.

evidence.text: Net cash used for investing activities of continuing operations in the first three months of 2019 primarily reflects capital expenditures of $16.8 million and cash paid for the Aquion and Pelican acquisitions of $287.2 million, net of cash acquired.
evidence.subsection: Investing activities

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increased capital expenditures support productivity and growth, indicating a positive trend.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is improving due to lower debt levels.

evidence.text: The 45.9 percent decrease in net interest expense in the first quarter of 2019 from 2018 was primarily driven by: •the impact of lower debt levels during the first quarter of 2019, compared to the comparable periods in 2018. In June 2018, the proceeds from the Separation were utilized to repay the remaining $255.3 million aggregate principal amount of our 2.9% fixed rate senior notes due 2018 and for the early extinguishment of €363.4 million aggregate principal amount of our 2.45% senior notes due 2019.
evidence.subsection: Net interest expense

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Significant reduction in debt levels leading to improved financial health.

factors.summary: Free cash flow is negative due to increased net working capital.

evidence.text: Cash used for operating activities of continuing operations was $257.1 million in the first three months of 2019, compared to $194.0 million in the same period of 2018. The $257.1 million in net cash used for operating activities of continuing operations in the first three months of 2019 primarily reflects an increase in net working capital.
evidence.subsection: Operating activities

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Negative free cash flow due to increased net working capital indicates cash flow strain.

factors.summary: Acquisitions are creating value through growth and market expansion.

evidence.text: In February 2019, as part of Filtration Solutions, we completed the acquisitions of Aquion and Pelican for $164.7 million and $122.5 million in cash, net of cash acquired, respectively. Aquion offers a diverse line of water conditioners, water filters, drinking-water purifiers, ozone and ultraviolet disinfection systems, reverse osmosis systems and acid neutralizers for the residential and commercial water treatment industry. Pelican provides residential whole home water treatment systems.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Acquisitions are creating value through growth and market expansion.

In addition, we have seen moderating growth in several of our end markets, primarily in our Filtration Solutions end markets in Europe.

factors.summary: Labor relations are stable with no active disputes or strikes.

evidence.text: We are comprised of three reporting segments: Aquatic Systems, Filtration Solutions and Flow Technologies. For the first three months of 2019, the Aquatic Systems, Filtration Solutions and Flow Technologies segments represented approximately 32%, 35% and 33% of total revenues, respectively.
